title: 2) Local effect with needle stimulation
text:
Local stimulation with a micro-trauma  is one of the main acupuncture effects on local
10
tissue healing. When the needle makes a reversible micro-injury on the local tissue, our body
activates the immune system around that injury and more blood and nutrient gather around
that tissue. It accelerates connective tissue reformation, like collagen ﬁbers or elastin.
One study showed that acupuncture on injured ligament increased the collagen ﬁbers on
local area. Another study in 2012 (N. Donoyama, et al.)  showed that acupuncture on the
11
face increased skin revitalization. They used local face locations:  BL-1,  GB-1,  ST-1,  ST-3,
ST-4,  ST-7,  SI-19,  CV-24,  Ex-HN-3,  and  Ex-HN-4,  and  compared  water  contents  and
appearance.
Many cosmetic acupuncture techniques like putting many needles transversely in very
small wrinkle spaces are based on this principle. 3-10 needles are put at one side of the
nasolabial fold by transverse insertion to maximize stimulation on the target dermis. The
roller needle and transplant needle are same. We make more small injuries in the dermis with
the roller, or longer lasting injuries with transplant needles.
Lee JA, et al. Acupuncture accelerates wound healing in burn-injured mice. Burns, 2011;37(1):117-25.
10
Nozomi Donoyama, et al. Cosmetic acupuncture to enhance facial skin appearance: a preliminary study. Acupuncture in
11
Medicine, 2012 Jun;30(2):152-153.

Needling by local effect with needle stimulation
Needling following the nasolabial folds
•To make a reversible micro-injury
•To induce the local tissue healing
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
